engineIntelligence.h2: Fiat Duna Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Fiat Duna in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Fiat Duna 149 C1.000 engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Fuel injection rough idle - MPI system sensors degrade causing unstable running after 70k miles.
Timing belt failure - belt snap on neglected units can cause valve damage on interference design.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,200-£2,400 (supply only) Estimated from comparable Sevel 1.6 units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Fiat Duna 146 A5.000 engine - petrol 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Carburettor wear - Weber carb jets clog causing poor idle and fuel consumption on high-mileage units.
Head gasket failure - overheating from clogged radiators leads to gasket breach on 1.3 units.

cards.altText: Fiat Duna 146 B2.000 engine - diesel 1.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector pump wear - mechanical injection pump loses pressure causing hard starting after 100k miles.
Glow plug circuit failure - cold-start difficulty from corroded wiring harness on pre-1990 units.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,500-£2,800 (supply only) Estimated from comparable 146 B diesel units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Fiat Duna Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Fiat Duna across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.1L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level 1.1L unit for base Duna models, simple carburettor design, reliable but underpowered for the chassis.
commonFailures: Carburettor jet clogging - poor idle and stalling after 60k miles on neglected units
Oil seal wear - minor external leaks common on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for 146 A2.000 Fiat Duna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: The most common Duna engine, 1.3L 8v carburettor unit offering balanced performance and simplicity for classic Fiat owners.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - overheating from clogged radiators causes gasket breach on high-mileage units
Carburettor wear - Weber carb requires regular adjustment, jets clog causing rough running
Timing belt neglect - snap on interference design causes valve damage if not replaced at 60k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for 146 A5.000 Fiat Duna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Updated 1.3L variant with revised emissions equipment for later Duna models, mechanically similar to 146 A5.000.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup causes rough idle on emissions-equipped units
Exhaust manifold cracking - thermal stress on cast manifold common after 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for 146 A8.000 Fiat Duna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Sevel-developed 1.6L fuel-injected engine, the most sought-after Duna powerplant for its improved driveability and refinement.
compatibleVariants: Duna 1.6 i.e., Duna Weekend 1.6 i.e., Duna Selecta 1.6
commonFailures: MPI sensor failure - throttle position and MAP sensors degrade causing rough idle after 70k miles
Timing belt failure - belt snap on interference design causes catastrophic valve damage if neglected
Fuel pump wear - in-tank pump loses pressure causing hard starting on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 149 C1.000 Fiat Duna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol (Brazilian)
entries.history: Brazilian-market 1.6L variant of the Sevel engine, fitted to Prêmio and later Duna imports, mechanically similar to European 149 C1.000.
compatibleVariants: Duna Prêmio 1.6, Duna 1.6 (Brazilian import), Duna Weekend 1.6 (Brazilian)
commonFailures: Fuel injection rough idle - MPI system sensors degrade causing unstable running after 70k miles
Timing belt failure - belt snap on interference design causes valve damage if not replaced at 60k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for BF 172 Fiat Duna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol MPI (Brazilian)
entries.history: Later Brazilian-market 1.6L with updated multi-point injection system, fitted to late-production Duna/Prêmio models.
compatibleVariants: Duna 1.6 MPI (Brazilian import), Duna Prêmio 1.6 MPI
commonFailures: MPI sensor failure - throttle position and MAP sensors degrade causing rough idle after 70k miles
Fuel pump wear - in-tank pump loses pressure causing hard starting on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for BF 175 Fiat Duna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Brazilian Fiasa-family 1.4L engine, occasionally fitted to later Duna/Prêmio models as an economical alternative to the 1.6L.
commonFailures: Carburettor wear - simple carb requires regular adjustment, jets clog causing poor running
Oil consumption - valve guide wear causes increased oil burn on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for Fiasa 1.4 Fiat Duna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.7L Diesel
entries.history: Naturally aspirated 1.7L diesel derived from the Fiat Uno diesel, offering excellent fuel economy but modest performance for the Duna chassis.
compatibleVariants: Duna 1.7 D, Duna Weekend 1.7 D, Duna DS 1.7
commonFailures: Injector pump wear - mechanical injection pump loses pressure causing hard starting after 100k miles
Glow plug circuit failure - cold-start difficulty from corroded wiring harness on pre-1990 units
Head gasket failure - overheating from clogged EGR causes gasket breach on high-mileage diesel units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 146 B2.000 Fiat Duna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.7L Diesel
entries.history: Updated variant of the 1.7L diesel with revised injection timing for later Duna models, mechanically similar to 146 B2.000.
commonFailures: Injector pump wear - mechanical injection pump loses pressure causing hard starting after 100k miles
Exhaust manifold cracking - thermal stress on cast manifold common after 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for 149 B3.000 Fiat Duna engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Fiat Duna Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Fiat Duna's engines are generally robust when maintained, but the 1.3 carburettor and 1.6 i.e. Sevel units dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your imported Duna.

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Why Fiat Duna 1.3 & 1.6 Engines Overheat
problems.rootCause: Clogged radiator or failed thermostat causes overheating; aluminium cylinder head warps, breaching the head gasket and allowing coolant into combustion chambers.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Flush cooling system, replace thermostat, pressure test radiator, new coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents overheating if done before gasket fails; not viable once gasket breached

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, machine surface if needed, fit new gasket set, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/head undamaged; risk of recurrence if root cause not addressed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded gasket, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Duna; eliminates recurrence risk, includes warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Fiat Duna worth £1,000-£4,000, a full rebuilt engine often offers better long-term value than a head gasket repair, especially if the car has exceeded 100k miles. We recommend replacement if the block shows signs of corrosion or if the head has warped beyond machining limits.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 149 C1.000 engine prices for your Fiat Duna

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Risk on Fiat Duna Interference Engines
problems.affectedModels: All Fiat Duna petrol engines (1987-2000, 146 A5.000, 149 C1.000, BF 172)
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belt perishes with age; if it snaps on these interference-design engines, pistons strike open valves causing severe internal damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idler pulleys; reset service interval
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; must be done every 60k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with new belt kit, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if belt has snapped and caused valve/piston damage; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Duna's timing belt history is unknown or it has exceeded 60k miles since last change, replace the belt kit immediately. If the belt has already snapped, a rebuilt engine is typically more cost-effective than attempting a valve repair on these rare import engines.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Duna timing belt replacement or rebuilt engine

problems.h4: Fuel System Issues - Carburettor vs Injection Problems on Fiat Duna
problems.affectedModels: Duna 1.3 carburettor (146 A5.000), Duna 1.6 i.e. MPI (149 C1.000, BF 172)
problems.rootCause: Carburettor jets clog from old fuel; MPI sensors (TPS, MAP) degrade causing incorrect fueling; both lead to rough idle, stalling, and poor economy.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean/adjust carburettor or replace MPI sensors, flush fuel lines, new filter
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for early symptoms; requires specialist knowledge of rare Fiat systems

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuild carburettor or replace full MPI kit, new fuel pump, injectors cleaned
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; parts availability can delay repairs for imports

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated fuel system components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if fuel issues are symptomatic of wider engine wear; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: For classic Duna owners, a fuel system service is often the first step. However, if the engine has high mileage or shows signs of internal wear, a rebuilt engine with updated fuel components offers better reliability and peace of mind for a rare import.
problems.cta: Compare Fiat Duna fuel system repair vs rebuilt engine costs

problems.h4: Oil Pressure Loss - Bearing Wear on High-Mileage Fiat Duna Engines
problems.affectedModels: All Fiat Duna engines, particularly 1.3 and 1.6 petrol units with 100k+ miles
problems.rootCause: Oil pump wear or bearing clearance increase from prolonged oil change neglect; low pressure causes accelerated wear and potential seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drop sump, replace oil pump, check/main bearings, reassemble with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/crank undamaged; requires engine-out access on Duna chassis

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with new bearings, oil pump, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for high-mileage Duna; eliminates recurrence risk, includes warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Duna's oil pressure warning light illuminates, stop driving immediately. For engines over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine is typically more cost-effective than attempting a bottom-end repair on these rare import units.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Duna low oil pressure engine replacement

problems.h4: Diesel Injector Pump Failure - Hard Starting on Fiat Duna 1.7D
problems.rootCause: Mechanical injection pump wear from contaminated fuel or neglected filter changes; loss of injection pressure causes hard starting and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove pump, send to specialist for rebuild, refit and bleed fuel system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; long lead times for rare Fiat diesel pump specialists

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel unit with new pump, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for high-mileage Duna diesel; includes warranty, avoids pump sourcing delays

problems.recommendation: Given the rarity of Fiat Duna diesel engines in the UK, a rebuilt engine often offers better value and faster turnaround than waiting for a specialist pump rebuild. We recommend replacement if the engine has exceeded 120k miles.
problems.cta: Compare Fiat Duna 1.7 diesel rebuilt engine prices

engineSizes.h2: Fiat Duna Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Fiat Duna is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Fiat Duna 1.1L Petrol Engines
items.description: The smallest petrol option for the Duna, the 1.1L unit powered base CS models in the late 1980s. Simple carburettor design made it easy to maintain but underpowered for the chassis, leading many owners to consider 1.3L swaps.
commonFailurePoints: minor oil seal leaks on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Fiat Duna 1.1L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Duna 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common Duna engine, the 1.3L 8v carburettor unit offered balanced performance and simplicity. Found across saloon and Weekend variants, it remains the most frequently searched replacement engine for UK import owners.
items.cta: Compare Fiat Duna 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Duna 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L Fiasa-family engine appeared on later Brazilian-market Duna/Prêmio models as an economical alternative to the 1.6L. Rare in the UK but sought after by owners seeking a modernised replacement for the 1.3L.
commonFailurePoints: valve guide wear on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Fiat Duna 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Duna 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: A Brazilian-market exclusive, the 1.5L petrol engine was fitted to Prêmio and early Duna imports. Mechanically similar to the 1.3L but with increased displacement for improved torque.
commonFailurePoints: oil consumption from valve guide wear
items.cta: Compare Fiat Duna 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Duna 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship petrol engine for the Duna, the 1.6L Sevel fuel-injected unit powered the 1.6 i.e., Weekend and Selecta variants. Its improved refinement and power make it the most sought-after replacement engine for performance-minded restorers.
commonFailurePoints: fuel pump wear on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Duna 1.6L petrol engines

items.title: Fiat Duna 1.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The only diesel option for the Duna, the 1.7L naturally aspirated unit offered excellent fuel economy but modest performance. Rare in the UK but valued by classic diesel enthusiasts for its simplicity and reliability when maintained.
commonFailurePoints: head gasket failure from EGR clogging
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Duna 1.7L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Duna 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: A very late and rare option, the 1.9L diesel appeared on final-production Duna/Elba models in South America. Extremely scarce in the UK; replacement engines require specialist sourcing from Brazilian or Argentine suppliers.
commonFailurePoints: turbocharger failure on rare turbo variants
items.cta: Enquire about Fiat Duna 1.9L diesel engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: Fiat Duna Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Fiat Duna engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.3 carburettor to the refined 1.6 i.e. Sevel injection.

items.title: Fiat Duna Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol engines dominate the Duna range, from the simple 1.3L carburettor unit to the refined 1.6L Sevel fuel-injected engine that powers the most sought-after Weekend and Selecta variants.
items.descriptor: Petrol engines dominate the Duna range, from the simple 1.3L carburettor unit to the refined 1.6L Sevel fuel-injected engine that powers the most sought-after Weekend and Selecta variants.
items.familiesLabel: Common Fiat Duna Petrol Engine Families
families: 146 A5.000 - 1.3L 8v carburettor, most common replacement engine for early Duna models
149 C1.000 - 1.6L Sevel fuel injection, flagship engine for Weekend/Selecta with improved refinement
BF 172 - Brazilian-market 1.6L variant, mechanically similar to European 149 C1.000
knownFor: Simple carburettor design on 1.3L makes DIY maintenance accessible for classic owners
1.6L Sevel injection offers smoother running but requires specialist diagnostics for MPI faults
Timing belt replacement critical on all petrol units - interference design risks valve damage if neglected

items.title: Fiat Duna Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.7L naturally aspirated diesel was the only oil-burning option for the Duna, offering excellent fuel economy but modest performance that limited its appeal in the UK import market.
items.descriptor: The 1.7L naturally aspirated diesel was the only oil-burning option for the Duna, offering excellent fuel economy but modest performance that limited its appeal in the UK import market.

families: 146 B2.000 - 1.7L mechanical diesel, most common diesel code for UK Duna imports
149 B3.000 - Updated 1.7L variant with revised injection timing for later models
knownFor: Excellent fuel economy (50+ mpg) made it popular for high-mileage use in South America
Mechanical injection pump requires specialist knowledge for diagnosis and repair in the UK
Glow plug circuit corrosion on pre-1990 units causes cold-start difficulty in UK winters

engineYears.h2: Fiat Duna Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Fiat Duna's production timeline (1985-2000), organised so UK import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The Duna launched with simple carburettor petrol engines and the 1.7L diesel, establishing the mechanical foundation for the model's reputation.
keyChanges: Duna saloon introduced in UK/Europe as Fiat Uno-based sedan derivative
1.3L carburettor petrol (146 A5.000) and 1.7L diesel (146 B2.000) established as core engines
Weekend estate variant added in 1987 with shared powertrains
knownFor: Simple carburettor design makes DIY maintenance accessible for classic Fiat enthusiasts
Timing belt replacement critical - neglect causes catastrophic valve damage on interference engines
Head gasket vulnerability on 1.3L if cooling system not maintained
years.cta: Get quotes for 1987-1991 Fiat Duna replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Duna 1.3 engine replacement • Fiat Duna head gasket failure • Fiat Duna timing belt cost

years.description: The 1991 facelift introduced the refined 1.6L Sevel fuel-injected engine, transforming the Duna's driving character and creating the most sought-after variant for UK restorers.
keyChanges: 1.6L Sevel fuel-injected engine (149 C1.000) introduced across saloon and Weekend
Selecta automatic variant launched with CVT transmission paired to 1.6L engine
Emissions equipment updated for European market compliance
mainEngines: 146 A8.000 - Updated 1.3L petrol with revised emissions (late production)
149 B3.000 - Updated 1.7L diesel with revised injection timing
knownFor: 1.6L Sevel engine offers significantly smoother running and better refinement than carburettor units
MPI system requires specialist diagnostics; sensor failures cause rough idle on high-mileage examples
Selecta CVT complexity means engine/gearbox units often sought together for replacement
years.cta: Get quotes for 1991-1995 Fiat Duna replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Duna 1.6 i.e. engine replacement • Fiat Duna Selecta engine • Fiat Duna MPI sensor failure

years.description: Final production years focused on Brazilian/Argentine markets with BF-series engine codes; UK imports from this period require careful code verification for compatibility.
keyChanges: Production shifted exclusively to South America after European sales ended
BF-series engine codes (BF 172, BF 175) introduced for Brazilian-market Duna/Prêmio
1.4L Fiasa engine offered as economical alternative in base models
mainEngines: BF 172 - Brazilian 1.6L Sevel variant (mechanically similar to 149 C1.000)
BF 175 - Updated Brazilian 1.6L with revised MPI system
Fiasa 1.4 - Economical 1.4L petrol for base Prêmio/Duna models
knownFor: Brazilian BF-series engines mechanically compatible with European 149 C1.000 but require ECU verification
Parts availability for late Brazilian models can be challenging in the UK; specialist sourcing recommended
High-mileage examples from South America may have experienced different maintenance standards
years.cta: Enquire about Brazilian-market Fiat Duna engine availability

faq.h2: Fiat Duna Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Fiat Duna engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Fiat Duna engine prices range from £900 for a 1.3L unit to £2,800 for a rare 1.7L diesel, depending on engine code, variant and supplier. Used engines start around £400 but carry higher risk for these niche imports.
keyPoints: 1.3L petrol rebuilt: £900-£1,800 supply only
1.6L Sevel rebuilt: £1,200-£2,400 supply only
1.7L diesel rebuilt: £1,500-£2,800 supply only (scarcity premium)
Supply & fit adds £400-£900 depending on garage location
Brazilian BF-series engines may carry additional sourcing costs
items.cta: Compare Fiat Duna engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Can I fit a Fiat Uno engine in a Fiat Duna?
items.answer: Yes, many Fiat Uno engines are compatible with the Duna as both share the Type 146 platform. The 1.3L 146 A5.000 and 1.7L diesel 146 B2.000 codes are directly interchangeable between Uno and Duna models.
keyPoints: Duna and Uno share Type 146 platform and engine mounting points
146 A5.000 (1.3L) and 146 B2.000 (1.7D) codes fit both models directly
1.6L Sevel 149 C1.000 is Duna-specific; verify ECU compatibility before swapping
Always confirm engine code and variant year before purchasing a donor engine
items.cta: Check Fiat Uno engine compatibility for your Fiat Duna

items.question: What is the most reliable Fiat Duna engine?
items.answer: The 1.6L Sevel fuel-injected engine (149 C1.000) is widely regarded as the most reliable Duna powerplant, offering better refinement and fewer carburettor-related issues than the 1.3L unit when properly maintained.
keyPoints: 149 C1.000 Sevel engine benefits from fuel injection precision and robust bottom end
Regular timing belt changes (every 60k miles) critical for all Duna petrol engines
Diesel 1.7L units are robust but rare parts availability complicates repairs in the UK
Brazilian BF 172 engines share Sevel design; verify ECU compatibility for UK use
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 149 C1.000 Fiat Duna engines

items.question: How long does a Fiat Duna engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: Well-maintained Fiat Duna engines typically reach 100,000-150,000 miles before major overhaul is needed. The 1.6L Sevel unit often exceeds 150k miles with timely belt changes and oil services.
keyPoints: Timing belt replacement every 60k miles or 5 years prevents catastrophic failure
Regular oil changes (every 6k miles) critical for bearing longevity on all variants
Cooling system maintenance prevents head gasket failure on 1.3L and 1.6L units
Diesel 1.7L units benefit from injector pump servicing every 80k miles
items.cta: Find a rebuilt Fiat Duna engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Fiat Duna?
items.answer: For a Fiat Duna in good body condition valued at £1,000-£4,000, engine replacement is often worthwhile for enthusiasts and classic collectors. A rebuilt engine with warranty typically costs less than the car's restored value.
keyPoints: Duna Weekend and Selecta variants command premium values in classic Fiat circles
Rebuilt engine cost (£900-£2,800) often less than car's restored market value
Supply & fit service available nationwide through specialist classic Fiat garages
Consider total restoration budget before committing to engine replacement
items.cta: Compare Fiat Duna engine replacement vs vehicle value

items.question: Where can I buy a replacement Fiat Duna engine in the UK?
items.answer: Specialist import breakers, classic Fiat parts suppliers and EngineMarket's network of UK engine specialists are the best sources for Fiat Duna engines. Avoid generic breakers unfamiliar with Brazilian/European code differences.
keyPoints: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists experienced with imported Fiats
Specialist breakers hold stock of rare 1.7L diesel and Sevel 1.6L units
Brazilian BF-series engines may require sourcing from South American suppliers
Always request compression test report and warranty terms before purchase
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Fiat Duna engine specialists today

items.question: Where is the engine number located on a Fiat Duna?
items.answer: The Fiat Duna engine code is typically stamped on the cylinder block near the gearbox bellhousing, or on a plate attached to the cylinder head below the inlet manifold. Clean the area to read the full code.
keyPoints: Check block near gearbox mating surface for stamped code (e.g., 149 C1.000)
Cylinder head plate may show code on later Sevel-injected models
Brazilian BF-series codes may appear on separate identification tag
Photograph code before cleaning to avoid losing stamped characters
items.cta: Identify your Fiat Duna engine code for accurate replacement quotes

items.question: How long does Fiat Duna engine delivery take in the UK?
items.answer: Most UK-supplied Fiat Duna engines are delivered within 3-5 working days via pallet courier. Rare Brazilian BF-series or 1.7L diesel units may require 7-14 days for specialist sourcing from European or South American suppliers.
keyPoints: Standard 1.3L and 1.6L petrol engines often in UK stock for next-day dispatch
Diesel and Brazilian-spec engines may require extended lead times
Pallet delivery to UK mainland included in most specialist quotes
Allow extra time for customs clearance on engines sourced outside the UK
items.cta: Check Fiat Duna engine availability and delivery times

items.question: Do you offer supply and fit for Fiat Duna engines near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket's network includes specialist classic Fiat garages across the UK offering supply & fit services for Duna engines. Enter your postcode to find the nearest accredited fitter for your variant.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £400-£900 to engine price depending on location
Specialist garages familiar with imported Fiat wiring and ECU systems recommended
Road test and post-fit check included in professional fitting service
Warranty validity may require fitting by accredited specialist - confirm terms
items.cta: Find Fiat Duna engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: Should I choose a rebuilt or used Fiat Duna engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Fiat Duna engines offer better reliability and warranty coverage (12 months typical) for classic restorations, while used engines (£400-£1,200) suit budget repairs on lower-value cars where short-term reliability is acceptable.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include new gaskets, bearings and timing components with bench testing
Used engines require compression test verification and carry shorter warranties (3-6 months)
For cars valued over £2,000, rebuilt engine cost often justified by reliability and warranty
Brazilian BF-series engines: rebuilt preferred due to parts scarcity for future repairs
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Fiat Duna engine prices and warranties
